title: How to Setup Live Chat Widget in GoHighLevel — Boost Conversions
description: Learn how to setup a live chat widget in GoHighLevel to engage visitors instantly and boost conversions. Step-by-step guide with best practices.
html_content:
Your website visitors are asking questions right now—and if you're not there to answer them instantly, they're leaving. That's where a live chat widget changes everything.

A live chat widget in GoHighLevel lets you engage prospects the moment they land on your site, answer their objections in real-time, and convert more visitors into qualified leads. Whether you're running an agency or managing client websites, this feature is a conversion machine when set up correctly.

In this guide, I'll walk you through the complete setup process, show you two deployment methods, and share the best practices top agencies use to maximize engagement and conversions. Ready to turn your website into a lead-capturing powerhouse? Let's get started.

📋 In This Guide

  • How to Create a Live Chat Widget in GoHighLevel
  • Customizing Your Chat Widget for Brand Consistency
  • Two Methods to Deploy Your Chat Widget
  • Setting Up Chat Notifications and Automations
  • Best Practices to Boost Visitor Engagement and Conversions

How to Create a Live Chat Widget in GoHighLevel

The first step is straightforward. Log into your GoHighLevel account and navigate to the Chat Widget section. You'll find this in your main dashboard—look for "Integrations" or "Communications" depending on your menu layout.

Click "Create New Widget" or the "+" button to start. GoHighLevel will prompt you to name your widget—use something descriptive like "Website Chat" or "Lead Capture Chat" so you can identify it later if you manage multiple widgets.

Once created, you'll see a blank widget ready for configuration. This is where the magic happens. Your widget needs to:

  • Assign it to a specific location or funnel page
  • Route incoming chats to the right team member or department
  • Set availability hours (optional, but recommended)
  • Create an offline message for after-hours inquiries

The widget creation process in GoHighLevel is designed to be non-technical. You won't need to write code or hire a developer—everything is built into the platform.

Customizing Your Chat Widget for Brand Consistency

A generic-looking chat widget won't perform well. Your visitors need to see your brand when they chat with you, not a cookie-cutter solution.

In the widget settings, you'll find the Customization or Appearance section. Here's what you can control:

  • Chat bubble color: Match your brand's primary color
  • Header text and color: "Chat with us" or a custom greeting
  • Chat window background: Customize the message area
  • Agent avatar: Add a profile photo for your team members
  • Welcome message: Set a greeting that displays when the chat opens
  • Button position: Bottom right, bottom left, or custom placement

💡 Pro Tip

Keep your welcome message short and action-focused. Instead of "Hi, how can we help?" try "Got questions? Ask us anything—we're here to help!" The more conversational, the higher your engagement rates.

Don't skip the avatar step. Adding a real face to your chat widget increases trust and makes conversations feel more personal. Visitors are more likely to respond to a real person than a generic bot icon.

Two Methods to Deploy Your Chat Widget

Once your widget is created and customized, you need to get it live on your website or funnel. GoHighLevel gives you two simple methods—choose the one that works best for your setup.

Method 1: Embed Code (For Any Website)

This is the most flexible method. After creating your widget, look for the "Embed Code" or "Installation Code" button. GoHighLevel will generate a snippet of JavaScript code that looks something like this:

\<script src="https://..." data-widget-id="..."\>\</script\>

Copy this code and paste it into the footer of your website (just before the closing `` tag). If you're using WordPress, you can use a code injection plugin or add it directly to your theme's footer.php file. If you're using a page builder like Elementor or Divi, use a custom HTML block.

The advantage here? It works on literally any website—custom-built sites, WordPress, Shopify, Webflow, you name it.

Method 2: Native Integration (For GoHighLevel Funnels and Sites)

If you've built your funnel or landing page directly in GoHighLevel, the widget integrates natively. Simply:

  1. Open your funnel or page in the editor
  2. Go to Settings and find Chat Widget
  3. Select your widget from the dropdown
  4. Publish your page

That's it. No code needed. The widget appears automatically on your page.

For agencies managing client sites, this native integration is a time-saver. You can set up multiple widgets across different client funnels in minutes.

Setting Up Chat Notifications and Automations

A chat widget is only valuable if you actually respond to visitors. GoHighLevel's automation features ensure you never miss a lead, even if you're not glued to your computer.

In the widget settings, set up notifications so you're alerted instantly when someone starts chatting. You can configure:

  • In-app notifications: See chat alerts within GoHighLevel
  • Email notifications: Get pinged to your email inbox
  • SMS alerts: Receive text messages for new chats (premium feature)
  • Team routing: Assign chats to specific team members based on availability

Next, create automation workflows triggered by chat interactions. For example:

  • If a visitor asks about pricing → Send them your pricing sheet automatically
  • If a visitor requests a demo → Create a calendar link and send it
  • If a chat goes unanswered for 5 minutes → Trigger an offline message and schedule a callback
  • If a visitor books a demo via chat → Send a confirmation email and calendar invite

These automations convert passive chat interactions into active lead nurturing. A visitor doesn't need to wait for your response—they get immediate value, which dramatically increases conversion rates.

Best Practices to Boost Visitor Engagement and Conversions

Setup is one thing. Actually converting visitors with your chat widget is another. Here are the strategies top agencies use:

1. Show the widget on your most valuable pages

Don't blast the chat widget on every page. Deploy it strategically on high-intent pages—pricing pages, product pages, and contact pages. These are where your most qualified visitors hang out.

2. Use a conversational welcome message

"Welcome to our chat" is boring. Try: "Have a question about our services? I can help—type away!" Conversational language increases response rates significantly.

3. Set clear availability hours

If you're not available 24/7, be upfront about it. A visitor is more likely to leave their info for a callback if they know when you'll respond. Transparency builds trust.

4. Train your team on chat best practices

Response time matters. Aim for under 2 minutes. Keep answers concise but helpful. Use the visitor's name in responses (GoHighLevel can auto-populate this). End with a clear call-to-action—"Ready to book a demo?" or "Want me to send you our case studies?"

5. Use chat data to improve your website

Review chat transcripts monthly. What questions are visitors asking repeatedly? Add FAQ sections to your pages. What objections keep coming up? Address them in your copy. Your chat widget is also a feedback tool.

Setting up a live chat widget in GoHighLevel is one of the fastest wins for agencies and businesses serious about conversions. Stop watching visitors leave without talking to you. Deploy your chat widget today, customize it for your brand, and watch your conversion rates climb. Thousands of agencies are already doing this—your competitors included. The question is: are you ready to catch up?
